title: SBOM attestation completeness — critical vendors without signed SBOM
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the content of the SBOM (covered by 22.43.5); focuses on presence/signature.
evidence: Saved search 'sbom_attestation_completeness' (weekly) lists critical vendors without a valid signed SBOM.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EU CRA mandates SBOM provisioning and signature.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU-CRA Art.13 (Obligations of manufacturers) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.44.5: SBOM attestation completeness — critical vendors without signed SBOM.

compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.44.5' running on Vendor SBOM register (vendor_sbom.csv), signature-validation tooling outputs, VRM criticality., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 SR-11(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.44.5: SBOM attestation completeness — critical vendors without signed SBOM.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.44.5' running on Vendor SBOM register (vendor_sbom.csv), signature-validation tooling outputs, VRM criticality., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A critical vendor has no SBOM in the register; the UC fires weekly until resolved.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A critical vendor has a signed SBOM within policy cadence; no alert.

dataSources: Vendor SBOM register (vendor_sbom.csv), signature-validation tooling outputs, VRM criticality.
spl:
| inputlookup vrm_attestations.csv
| where criticality="critical"
| lookup vendor_sbom_attestations.csv vendor OUTPUT sbom_sha256 signed signed_by signed_at
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(sbom_sha256) OR signed!="true" OR age_days>180
| table vendor criticality sbom_sha256 signed signed_by age_days
description: Surfaces critical vendors whose SBOM is absent, unsigned, or older than policy cadence (default 180 days).
value: Makes the CRA/NIS2 SBOM requirement a tracked operational metric.
implementation: (1) Onboard vendor SBOMs through procurement workflow; (2) validate signatures with sigstore or equivalent; (3) run weekly. Lookups: `vendor_sbom_attestations`, `vendor_sbom_attestations.csv`, `vrm_attestations`, `vrm_attestations.csv`.
visualization: Table of at-risk vendors, bar chart of signature freshness, single-value 'critical vendors with signed SBOM'.
knownFalsePositives: Vendors mid-re-signing can appear stale; allow a 7-day grace after signed_attempted_at.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Vendor SBOM register (vendor_sbom.csv), signature-validation tooling outputs, VRM criticality..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Onboard vendor SBOMs through procurement workflow; (2) validate signatures with sigstore or equivalent; (3) run weekly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup vrm_attestations.csv
| where criticality="critical"
| lookup vendor_sbom_attestations.csv vendor OUTPUT sbom_sha256 signed signed_by signed_at
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(sbom_sha256) OR signed!="true" OR age_days>180
| table vendor criticality sbom_sha256 signed signed_by age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**SBOM attestation completeness — critical vendors without signed SBOM** — Makes the CRA/NIS2 SBOM requirement a tracked operational metric.

Documented **Data sources**: Vendor SBOM register (vendor_sbom.csv), signature-validation tooling outputs, VRM criticality. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Filters the current rows with `where criticality="critical"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(sbom_sha256) OR signed!="true" OR age_days>180` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_days, criticality, sbom_sha256, signed, signed_by, vendor.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of at-risk vendors, bar chart of signature freshness, single-value 'critical vendors with signed SBOM'.
grandmaExplanation: We check on surfaces critical vendors whose sbom is absent, unsigned, or older than policy cadence (default 180 days). This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
